entries.description:
Password input (HTML `type="password"`) with secure autocomplete defaults.

Defaults to the key `'password'` and label `'Password'` unless overridden.

Companion to `password-field` for sign-up flows. Defaults `autocomplete` to `new-password`. Pair with `password-with-verify-fields` for cross-field equality validation.

Defaults to the key `'verifyPassword'` and label `'Verify Password'` unless overridden.

Password + verify-password pair with cross-field equality validation wired up. Drop-in for sign-up flows.

The verify password field uses an expression-based custom validator that compares the
verify field value against the primary password field's value via `formValue`.

properties.name: password
properties.description: Optional configuration for the primary password field.

properties.name: verifyPassword
properties.description: Optional configuration for the verify/confirm password field.

entries.description:
Complete login/signup field set: username, password, and optional verify-password. Drop into the top-level `fields: []`.

When `verifyPassword` is provided, a second password field is added with a custom
validator that ensures both password values match.

properties.name: username
properties.description:
Username field configuration. Use `'email'` or `'username'` for defaults,
or provide a custom config.

properties.name: password
properties.description: Optional configuration for the password field.

properties.name: verifyPassword
properties.description:
Whether to include a verify password field, or a custom configuration for it.
Set to `true` for defaults, `false`/`undefined` to omit, or pass a config object.

entries.description:
Username field for login forms. Accepts `"email"` or `"username"` as shorthand presets, or a full config object.

Supports email or plain text input based on the provided configuration.

properties.name: email
properties.description: Configuration for an email-based username field.

properties.name: username
properties.description: Configuration for a plain text username field.

entries.description: Multi-checkbox group. Use for small static option sets where every option is visible at once.
entries.example: dbxForgeChecklistField<string>({ key: 'flags', props: { options: [{ value: 'a', label: 'A' }, { value: 'b', label: 'B' }] } })

entries.description:
Escape hatch — injects any Angular component as the field renderer via DbxInjection. Use when no existing form field fits.

Uses {@link DbxInjectionComponent} to dynamically inject any Angular component
into the form. Generates a unique key when none is provided so that ng-forge's
field reconciliation treats each config change as a new field instance.

properties.name: key
properties.description: Key for the field. Optional for display-only components.

properties.name: componentField (props)
properties.description: The injection component configuration that describes which component to render.

properties.name: allowDisabledEffects (props)
properties.description:
Whether to visually indicate the disabled state on this component.

Defaults to `true`. Set to `false` for display-only components that should
remain visually unchanged when the form is disabled.

entries.description:
Single-select dropdown over a static or async value list. Simpler than `source-select` when metadata lookup is unnecessary.

The component uses `<mat-form-field>` with `[formField]` for native ng-forge value binding,
proper Material rendering, and built-in logic (hidden/disabled/readonly) support.

Supports static arrays, Observable option sources, and `ValueSelectionOptionClear` entries.
entries.example: dbxForgeValueSelectionField<string>({ key: 'status', props: { options: [{ value: 'active', label: 'Active' }] } })

properties.name: options (props)
properties.description:
Options to select from.

Accepts a static array or an Observable that emits option arrays.
Options may include {@link ValueSelectionOptionClear} entries with `clear: true`.

properties.name: addClearOption (props)
properties.description:
When true or a string, adds a clear/reset option at the top of the options list.
If a string is provided, it is used as the clear option label.

If the options already contain a clear option, no additional one is added.

entries.description:
Rich HTML text editor (ngx-editor). Output is the serialized HTML string.

Uses ngx-editor under the hood, outputting HTML format.
The field defaults to an empty string.

properties.name: minLength (props)
properties.description: Minimum text length.

properties.name: maxLength (props)
properties.description: Maximum text length.

properties.name: hint (props)
properties.description: Hint text shown below the editor.

entries.description:
Flex row that lays child fields out in columns. Child fields typically carry a `col` property (1–12) for grid placement.

Uses the `@ng-forge/dynamic-forms` `RowField` type with a 12-column grid system.
Each child field can specify a `col` value (1-12) for responsive sizing.
entries.example: dbxForgeRow({ fields: [ { ...dbxForgeTextField({ key: 'first' }), col: 6 }, { ...dbxForgeTextField({ key: 'last' }), col: 6 } ] })

properties.name: key
properties.description:
Optional key for the row. Defaults to a unique auto-generated key.

Must be unique within the form config to avoid ng-forge duplicate key errors.

entries.description:
Creates a plain ng-forge `group` field. A group produces a nested object in
the form value under its `key`, so the key is semantically significant and
must be chosen deliberately.

For visual-only grouping (conditional visibility, layout wrappers, shared
CSS class) that should NOT introduce a nested object in the form value, use
{@link dbxForgeContainer} instead — that is what ng-forge's `container`
field type is for.

For sections with headers, use the section wrapper type instead.

Wraps content fields in a Material slide toggle — the toggle state controls conditional visibility of the inner fields.

Uses ng-forge's built-in `toggle` field type (MatSlideToggle) and
`FieldValueCondition` for conditional visibility. The toggle boolean value
IS part of the form model. The hidden content is wrapped in a `container`
(not a `group`), so the wrapped fields sit at the same level as the toggle
in the form value — they are NOT nested under an extra object.

Structure produced:
```
Row (outer)
  ├── toggle field (type: 'toggle', boolean value)
  └── Container (content, hidden when toggle === false)
```

This is the forge equivalent of the formly `formlyToggleWrapper`.

properties.name: fields
properties.description: Fields to show/hide based on the toggle state.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Label for the toggle control.

properties.name: key
properties.description: Key for the toggle boolean field. Defaults to auto-generated `_toggle_N`.

properties.name: className
properties.description: Optional CSS class name applied to the outer row container.

properties.name: defaultOpen
properties.description: Whether the toggle starts in the open state. Defaults to false.

properties.name: contentKey
properties.description:
Optional key for the content container. Auto-generated if omitted.

The container does not add a property to the form value; this is only a
stable identifier for the container field.

entries.description:
Wraps content fields behind a button or text "expand" control. Use for optional sections like "Show advanced options".

Uses a custom `dbx-forge-expand` field type for the expand trigger and
`FieldValueCondition` for conditional visibility on the content. The
expand boolean value IS part of the form model. The hidden content is
wrapped in a `container` (not a `group`), so the wrapped fields sit at the
same level as the expand control in the form value — they are NOT nested
under an extra object.

Structure produced:
```
Row (outer)
  ├── expand field (type: 'dbx-forge-expand', boolean value)
  └── Container (content, hidden when expand field === false)
```

This is the forge equivalent of the formly `formlyExpandWrapper`.

properties.name: fields
properties.description: Fields to show/hide when the expand control is toggled.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Label for the expand trigger.

properties.name: buttonType
properties.description: Visual style for the expand trigger. Defaults to `'text'`.

properties.name: key
properties.description: Key for the expand boolean field. Defaults to auto-generated `_expand_N`.

properties.name: className
properties.description: Optional CSS class name applied to the outer row container.

properties.name: defaultOpen
properties.description: Whether the expand starts in the open state. Defaults to false.

properties.name: contentKey
properties.description:
Optional key for the content container. Auto-generated if omitted.

The container does not add a property to the form value; this is only a
stable identifier for the container field.

entries.description: Multi-select backed by a lazy-loadable custom list component. Use when you need complete control over item layout and pagination.
entries.example: dbxForgeListSelectionField<Item, MyListComponent, string>({ key: 'items', props: { listComponentClass, readKey: (i) => i.id, state$ } })

properties.name: listComponentClass (props)
properties.description: List component class to render items from. Can be provided as an Observable for lazy loading.

properties.name: readKey (props)
properties.description: Read key function to extract the identifier from each item.

properties.name: state$ (props)
properties.description: Observable that provides the items to select.

properties.name: loadMore (props)
properties.description: Function that signals to load more items.

properties.name: hint (props)
properties.description: Hint text shown below the field.

entries.generic: <T = unknown, C extends AbstractDbxSelectionListWrapperDirective<T> = AbstractDbxSelectionListWrapperDirective<T>, K extends PrimativeKey = PrimativeKey>

entries.description: Selection field rendering selected values as Material chips. Defaults to multi-select; flip to single-select via the underlying props.
entries.example: dbxForgePickableChipField<Tag>({ key: 'tags', label: 'Tags', props: { loadValues: () => loadTags$, displayForValue: displayTag } })
entries.generic: <T = unknown, M = unknown, H extends PrimativeKey = PrimativeKey>

entries.description: Scrollable-list variant of `pickable-chip` — same API, different presentation. Prefer this when the option set is large.
entries.generic: <T = unknown, M = unknown, H extends PrimativeKey = PrimativeKey>

entries.description:
String-value specialization of `searchable-chip`. `allowStringValues` is forced true — use for free-form tag entry.

Always sets `allowStringValues: true` on the inner field props so pressing Enter
(or typing a separator key) commits the typed value as a chip.
Multi-value autocomplete with chips. Defaults to multi-select; supports free-form text entry when `allowStringValues` is set.
entries.generic: <T = unknown, M = unknown, H extends PrimativeKey = PrimativeKey>

entries.description: Single-value autocomplete field with search-as-you-type. Optionally allows free-form typed strings as values.
entries.generic: <T = unknown, M = unknown, H extends PrimativeKey = PrimativeKey>

entries.description:
Selection field that stores just the value key (`T`) but resolves full metadata (`M`) async for display. Use for reference fields where the form should store only the id.

The component uses `<mat-form-field>` with `[formField]` for native ng-forge value binding,
proper Material rendering, and built-in logic (hidden/disabled/readonly) support.
entries.example: dbxForgeSourceSelectField<string, User>({ key: 'userId', props: { valueReader: (u) => u.id, metaLoader, displayForValue } })

entries.description:
Repeatable array wrapper with add/remove/drag-to-reorder controls. Template fields are cloned per item. Internally built with `dbxForgeFieldFunction` but categorized as a primitive because composites wrap it.

Wraps the array with {@link DbxForgeArrayFieldWrapperComponent} for label/hint,
and wraps each template item in a ContainerField with
{@link DbxForgeArrayFieldElementWrapperComponent} for per-item drag handle,
label, and remove button.

properties.name: template
properties.description:
Template defining the fields for each array item.
Each item is wrapped in a ContainerField with the element wrapper
for per-item drag handle, label, and remove button.

The array starts empty — items are added via the add button.

entries.description: Material slide toggle. Renders inside a styled outline box by default so it visually matches surrounding outlined form fields; pass `styledBox: false` to opt out.

properties.name: styledBox
properties.description:
Whether to render the toggle inside a styled outline box.

Defaults to `true`.

entries.description: Material checkbox. Shares the styled-outline-box opt-out with toggle.
entries.example: dbxForgeCheckboxField({ key: 'agree', label: 'I agree to the terms' })

properties.name: styledBox
properties.description:
Whether to render the checkbox inside a styled outline box.

Defaults to `true`.

entries.description:
Material datepicker (date-only, no time). For time-of-day picking use the `date-time` field; for ranges use `date-range-row` or `date-time-range-row`.

Uses the native ng-forge MatDatepickerField.
entries.example: dbxForgeDateField({ key: 'startDate', label: 'Start Date', required: true })

entries.description:
Two-column row of start/end date-time fields configured for date-only picking. Use when you need a paired start/end date range laid out horizontally.

Composite builder that creates a pair of date pickers for selecting a date range (start and end dates)
arranged in a flex row. The pickers are synchronized so the start date stays before the end date.

This is the forge equivalent of formly's `formlyDateRangeField()`.
entries.example: dbxForgeDateRangeRow({ required: true, start: { key: 'from', label: 'From' }, end: { key: 'to', label: 'To' } })

entries.description:
Combined date-time picker with timezone, value mode (DATE_STRING / TIMESTAMP / Date), and time mode (REQUIRED / OPTIONAL / NONE). Powers `date-range-row` and `date-time-range-row`.

Full parity with formly `dateTimeField()` — supports timezone, valueMode, timeMode,
pickerConfig, presets, and all other features via the `props` slot.

properties.name: openOnInputClick (props)
properties.description:
Behavior when the date input is clicked.

- `'picker'` — opens the datepicker overlay (default)
- `'input'` — normal text-selection / cursor-placement behavior

entries.description:
Two-column row of time-only pickers for selecting a time range within a single day.

This is the forge equivalent of formly's `formlyDateTimeRangeField()`.

Inline calendar-style date-range picker with fixed range length (e.g. "7 days from start"). Wrapped in a Material form-field container with a custom selection strategy.

Uses an inline `<mat-calendar>` with a custom selection strategy, matching the formly
`fixedDateRangeField()` behavior. Supports multiple selection modes, timezone conversion,
date range input configuration, and optional text inputs.

properties.name: dateRangeInput (props)
properties.description:
Date range input to build the date range from a single picked date.
Required for 'single' mode and boundary-based selection modes.

properties.name: selectionMode (props)
properties.description:
Selection mode to use when picking dates on the calendar.

- `'single'` — Picks one date, range computed from dateRangeInput config.
- `'normal'` — Standard start/end range picking with two clicks.
- `'arbitrary'` — Free-form range selection within a boundary.
- `'arbitrary_quick'` — Like arbitrary, but immediately sets the value on first click.

Defaults to `'single'`.

properties.name: valueMode (props)
properties.description:
Value mode for the dates in the output DateRange.
Defaults to DATE.

properties.name: fullDayInUTC (props)
properties.description: Whether to pass the date value as a UTC date, or a date in the current timezone.

properties.name: pickerConfig (props)
properties.description: Custom picker configuration (limits, schedule).

properties.name: timezone (props)
properties.description: The input timezone to default to. Ignored if fullDayInUTC is true.

properties.name: showTimezone (props)
properties.description: Whether to display the timezone. Defaults to true.

properties.name: presets (props)
properties.description: Custom presets.

properties.name: showRangeInput (props)
properties.description: Whether to show the range input text fields. Defaults to true.

properties.name: appearance (props)
properties.description: Material form field appearance.

properties.name: hint (props)
properties.description: Hint text displayed below the field.

entries.description:
Duration input with popover picker. Output shape varies by `valueMode` — number (ms/s/…), string, or structured object.

Uses a custom ng-forge ValueFieldComponent that provides a text input
accepting duration strings (e.g. "2h30m") and a popover picker.

properties.name: outputUnit
properties.description:
The unit of the output value.

Defaults to `'ms'` (milliseconds).

properties.name: valueMode
properties.description:
The output value mode.

Defaults to `'number'`.

properties.name: allowedUnits
properties.description: The time units available for the field.

properties.name: pickerUnits
properties.description:
Which units to show in the popover picker.

Defaults to allowedUnits filtered to exclude 'ms'.

properties.name: carryOver
properties.description: Whether the popover picker should carry over values to the next larger unit.

entries.description: Forge number field pre-configured for dollar amount input with cent-level precision. Pre-sets `transform.precision` to `DOLLAR_AMOUNT_PRECISION` so values round to whole cents.
entries.example: const field = dbxForgeDollarAmountField({ key: 'price', label: 'Price', min: 0, required: true });

properties.name: step
properties.description: Step increment for the input.

properties.name: enforceStep
properties.description:
When true, validates that the value is divisible by `step`.
Requires `step` to be set.

properties.name: idempotentTransform
properties.description:
An idempotent number transformation applied as a value parser (e.g., rounding, precision, bounds).

For non-idempotent transformations, you should directly configure the `transform` property instead.

entries.description:
Numeric input (HTML `type="number"`). Supports min/max/step constraints, optional step enforcement (divisibility validator), and idempotent number transforms.

When `step` is provided, sets the HTML `step` attribute on the input via `meta`.
When both `step` and `enforceStep` are set, adds a custom divisibility validator.
entries.example: dbxForgeNumberField({ key: 'quantity', label: 'Quantity', min: 1, max: 100, step: 1, enforceStep: true })

properties.name: step
properties.description: Step increment for the input.

properties.name: enforceStep
properties.description:
When true, validates that the value is divisible by `step`.
Requires `step` to be set.

properties.name: idempotentTransform
properties.description:
An idempotent number transformation applied as a value parser (e.g., rounding, precision, bounds).

For non-idempotent transformations, you should directly configure the `transform` property instead.

entries.description:
Material slider wrapped in a form-field container. Supports thumb label, tick interval, and step-derived tick spacing.

The wrapper provides the Material outlined form-field appearance (notched outline with
floating label, hint/error subscript). The inner slider uses the ng-forge built-in
`slider` type.
entries.example: dbxForgeNumberSliderField({ key: 'rating', label: 'Rating', min: 0, max: 10, step: 1 })

properties.name: thumbLabel
properties.description:
Whether or not to show the thumb label while sliding.

Defaults to true.

properties.name: tickInterval
properties.description:
Tick interval. If not provided defaults to the step value, if provided.
If false, the ticks are disabled.

entries.description:
International phone number input backed by ngx-mat-input-tel. Supports preferred-country lists, search, and optional extension input.

Uses the custom 'phone' field type which renders the ngx-mat-input-tel component
bridged to Signal Forms.

properties.name: preferredCountries
properties.description: Preferred countries to show at the top of the country selector.

properties.name: onlyCountries
properties.description: ISO country codes to restrict the dropdown to.

properties.name: enableSearch
properties.description: Whether or not to enable the search feature. True by default.

properties.name: allowExtension
properties.description: Whether or not to allow adding an extension. False by default.

properties.name: autocomplete
properties.description:
Sets the autocomplete attribute on the phone input.

Pass `'tel'` to enable phone autofill, or `false`/`'off'` to disable.

properties.name: preferredCountries (props)
properties.description: ISO country codes for countries shown first in the dropdown.

properties.name: onlyCountries (props)
properties.description: ISO country codes to restrict the dropdown to.

properties.name: enableSearch (props)
properties.description: Whether or not to enable the search feature. True by default.

properties.name: allowExtension (props)
properties.description: Whether or not to allow adding an extension. False by default.

properties.name: appearance (props)
properties.description: Material form field appearance.

properties.name: hint (props)
properties.description: Hint text displayed below the field.

properties.name: autocomplete (props)
properties.description:
Autocomplete value for the phone input. The underlying `ngx-mat-input-tel`
component supports `'off'` and `'tel'`.

entries.description: Pre-configured text field for capturing a full name with sensible min/max length defaults.
entries.example: dbxForgeNameField({ key: 'fullName', label: 'Full Name', required: true })

entries.description:
Text field pre-configured with email input type and email validator. Prefer this over configuring `dbxForgeTextField` with `inputType: "email"` directly.

Uses the `'email'` input type for built-in browser validation.

properties.name: autocomplete
properties.description: Sets the autocomplete attribute on the input. Pass `false` to disable browser autofill.

entries.description: City name input enforcing `ADDRESS_CITY_MAX_LENGTH`. Typically used inside the address composite set.

properties.name: inputType
properties.description: HTML input type. Defaults to `'text'`.

properties.name: idempotentTransform
properties.description:
An idempotent string transformation applied as a value parser (e.g., trim, uppercase).

For non-idempotent transformations, you should directly configure the `transform` property instead.

entries.description: US state input. When `asCode: true`, validates two-letter codes and auto-uppercases input via an idempotent transform.

properties.name: asCode
properties.description: When true, validates and formats as a 2-letter state code (e.g., `'CA'`).

entries.description: Country name input enforcing `ADDRESS_COUNTRY_MAX_LENGTH`. Typically used inside the address composite set.

properties.name: inputType
properties.description: HTML input type. Defaults to `'text'`.

properties.name: idempotentTransform
properties.description:
An idempotent string transformation applied as a value parser (e.g., trim, uppercase).

For non-idempotent transformations, you should directly configure the `transform` property instead.

entries.description: US zip code input with pattern validation and max-length enforcement.

properties.name: inputType
properties.description: HTML input type. Defaults to `'text'`.

properties.name: idempotentTransform
properties.description:
An idempotent string transformation applied as a value parser (e.g., trim, uppercase).

For non-idempotent transformations, you should directly configure the `transform` property instead.

entries.description: Latitude/longitude coordinate input with decimal-degree pattern validation.
Street address line input. The `line` prop controls which line (1 or 2) — it affects key and label generation.

properties.name: line
properties.description: Address line number: 0 for single "Street" line, 1 for "Line 1", 2 for "Line 2".

entries.description: Flat array of address fields (line(s), city, state, zip, optional country) with a sensible flex layout. Drop directly into a parent `fields: []`.

properties.name: required
properties.description:
Whether or not to make required fields required.

True by default.

properties.name: includeLine2
properties.description:
Whether or not to include the second address line.

True by default.

properties.name: includeCountry
properties.description:
Whether or not to include the country.

True by default.

entries.description: Wraps `address-fields` in a `GroupField` so the address is stored as a nested object under one key. Prefer this when the rest of the form doesn't want address fields flattened.

properties.name: required
properties.description:
Whether or not to make required fields required.

True by default.

properties.name: includeLine2
properties.description:
Whether or not to include the second address line.

True by default.

properties.name: includeCountry
properties.description:
Whether or not to include the country.

True by default.

entries.description: Repeatable array of addresses built on top of `array-field` + `address-group`. Keeps the `Field` suffix because it returns a single composite field whose value is an array of addresses.

properties.name: required
properties.description:
Whether or not to make required fields required.

True by default.

properties.name: includeLine2
properties.description:
Whether or not to include the second address line.

True by default.

properties.name: includeCountry
properties.description:
Whether or not to include the country.

True by default.

properties.name: maxAddresses
properties.description: Maximum number of addresses allowed. Defaults to 6.

entries.description: Single-line text input. Supports text/email/password input types, autocomplete attribute, regex pattern validation, and idempotent string transforms (trim, case changes, etc.).

properties.name: inputType
properties.description: HTML input type. Defaults to `'text'`.

properties.name: idempotentTransform
properties.description:
An idempotent string transformation applied as a value parser (e.g., trim, uppercase).

For non-idempotent transformations, you should directly configure the `transform` property instead.

entries.description: Multi-line textarea input. Supports row count, autocomplete attribute, pattern validation (RegExp → string conversion), and default value.

properties.name: rows
properties.description: Number of visible text rows. Defaults to 3.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.description: Initial value for the textarea. Defaults to empty string.

entries.description:
Creates a responsive flex layout container that arranges child fields horizontally
with configurable sizing, breakpoints, and responsive behavior.

Each child field gets a `dbx-flex-N` CSS class applied (merged with any existing className).
The container hosts the `dbx-forge-flex` wrapper, which renders the `dbxFlexGroup`
directive for responsive breakpoint handling.

A `container` is used (not a `group`) because flex layout is purely visual —
the wrapped fields should remain at the same level in the form value, not be
nested under an extra object.

This is the forge equivalent of {@link formlyFlexLayoutWrapper}.

properties.name: size
properties.description: Default flex size for fields that don't specify their own.

entries.description: Section wrapper config — attach via a field's `wrappers: []` array for a semantic section with header and optional card elevation.

properties.name: headerConfig
properties.description: Section header configuration.

properties.name: elevate
properties.description: Whether to apply elevated card styling to the section.

properties.name: subsection
properties.description: Whether to render as a `<dbx-subsection>` instead of `<dbx-section>`.

entries.description: Subsection variant of `section-wrapper` — defaults to heading level 4 and `subsection: true`.

properties.name: headerConfig
properties.description: Section header configuration.

properties.name: elevate
properties.description: Whether to apply elevated card styling to the section.

properties.name: subsection
properties.description: Whether to render as a `<dbx-subsection>` instead of `<dbx-section>`.

entries.description: Style wrapper config — applies dynamic CSS classes (`ngClass`) and/or inline styles (`ngStyle`) to any field via its `wrappers: []`.

properties.name: classGetter
properties.description: Observable or static value providing CSS class names via `[ngClass]`.

properties.name: styleGetter
properties.description: Observable or static value providing inline styles via `[ngStyle]`.
